pattern: Track, for every node, the XOR of one-hot letter bits along its path from the root; a path between two nodes can be rearranged into a palindrome exactly when the XOR of their two root-masks has at most one bit set, so counting pairs becomes a frequency-map lookup over 27 mask variants per node instead of walking every path.
hints: A multiset of characters can be rearranged into a palindrome exactly when at most one character appears an odd number of times -- track odd/even parity per letter instead of exact counts.

Represent the parity of every letter along the root-to-node path as one 26-bit mask (XOR toggles a bit each time that edge letter is crossed); the path between any two nodes has a parity mask equal to the XOR of their two root-masks, because the shared prefix up to their common ancestor cancels out.

Process nodes in an order where a node's mask is computed after its parent's; for each node, look up how many earlier nodes have the exact same mask (path is already balanced) or a mask differing in exactly one bit (one leftover odd letter), add both to the answer, then record this node's own mask in the frequency map before moving to the next.

edge_inputs.description: a straight-line path with five distinct edge letters
a star with a mix of repeated and unique leaf letters
a small binary tree where every edge shares the same letter
a single node has no pairs at all
two nodes, exactly one always-palindromic pair
a path with alternating letters where only some longer sub-paths balance out
a longer path with a repeating three-letter pattern
a star with 26 leaves each on a distinct letter, exercising every single-bit mask difference

stress.description: large random tree (each node's parent chosen from earlier nodes) with random lowercase edge letters
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100000))
    parent = [-1] + [rng.randint(0, i - 1) for i in range(1, n)]
    s = ''.join(chr(ord('a') + rng.randint(0, 25)) for _ in range(n))
    return [parent, s]

solutions.python:
class Solution:
    def countPalindromePaths(self, parent, s):
        n = len(parent)
        children = [[] for _ in range(n)]
        for i in range(1, n):
            children[parent[i]].append(i)
        mask = [0] * n
        order = [0]
        idx = 0
        while idx < len(order):
            u = order[idx]
            idx += 1
            for v in children[u]:
                bit = 1 << (ord(s[v]) - ord('a'))
                mask[v] = mask[u] ^ bit
                order.append(v)
        from collections import defaultdict
        freq = defaultdict(int)
        freq[0] = 1
        ans = 0
        for u in order[1:]:
            m = mask[u]
            ans += freq[m]
            for b in range(26):
                ans += freq[m ^ (1 << b)]
            freq[m] += 1
        return ans
